So this is accurate, and (A) seems to be true. Option (B) says, "In Holland people give presents to unbirthday persons on their birthdays." The passage mentions that in Holland, children not only get presents but also give things out to their classmates and teachers on their birthdays. The term "unbirthday persons" might be a bit confusing here, but if it's referring to people who are not the birthday child, then this part is correct. The passage does say that they give cakes, cookies, and candles to their classmates and teachers, which are other people not celebrating their birthday. So (B) could also be true. However, the term "unbirthday persons" isn't directly used in the passage, so I need to be careful. The passage says they give things to their classmates and teachers, not to "unbirthday persons."
